But i recently found some more skate vids and found that instaremix truly my passion.
Also i felt weird about having so much in the public on instagram, but for U skrrrtrs heres the link to my footage mainly from last winter if u ever feel like stepping into the archive and see what happened in the first winter in a while where i wasnt spoiled with a big indoor space every day. The claustrophobic systematic crank practice that made me long for moving in space so much that i had to increase my wheel size when spring came, just to make my rolls travel further

just like i enjoyed on jacks 22 when i tried it!

And there is my trial video finally! It took me a bid longer as I wanted because I tore my ankle. But finally was able to film the last clip for the video. The last couple of months I was super excited to do trials so me Stan and sometimes Lotte rode lots of trial just because we had more fun in trials then in riding flat or street. Defiantly does not main I wont do street or flat anymore. I just do what I want to do so if its flat or trails or whatever it's a sport for fun and to keep my sporty and physical okay! Enough blab la bla. Just click on the link and go watch my trials edit!

https://youtu.be/3mAF2n-NrgQ

Hey guys, I've come out with a new YouTube channel & I'm committing to it with 1 video every week (Thursday @ 5PM EST). Hoping I can give back to this community by providing some good and entertaining content that could expand the sport & potentially help beginners. If you guys have some time and want to support this adventure, please consider taking a look. Super excited about this! 🤩

 Let me know what you think and if there's any video ideas of content you guys want to see or think would bring more new riders in.

 ➡️  https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOKhnZGomjRoMgWBMTAt_Tg  ⬅️

https://youtu.be/PS6SIo7n-YU
🔥🔥NEW VIDEO🔥🔥

If you guys have any ideas for the up and coming weeks' videos please share. Looking to really up the quality in unicycling content for you guys ❤

Let me know if you guys have any feedback or comments about the video, as I'm really trying to push the sport out there more, which means trying to appeal to the general audience as well as the unicyclists out there. Thank you and enjoy 🙂

This is my latest session in the form of a vlog. 

Entirely filmed and edited on my new phone, Sony Xperia 1vi. Messed up several settings it seems and ended up with a house matching my neighbours mustard yellow one haha. 

First and last time I'll edit something this big on a phone... Was tedious to say the least! 

Hopefully some of you get some motivation/enjoyment out of it! 

https://youtu.be/Ka5q7QMATvg

Here's a recap of tonight's session, following my viewing of Sam's vlog.

https://youtu.be/_anpgk-flYk

Was inspired by Sam's varial fakieflips and backside tricks. Took a while to commit to the latest, but eventually got there.

It's obviously not much, but to land that out backside was a major milestone for me. Not for the particular trick in itself, but IIRC, it's been 11 years since I landed a new trick. Everything else since 2013 had been variations or lines using the same tricks. (Coincidently, I believe my previous latest trick was revflip, which I just relanded tonight 🤷)

Some riders chase personal new tricks or NBDs, which is truely great to see for all of us. I decided some time ago I'd stop that, probably because I was in a comfortable spot with my riding. I told myself I was happy with my modest bag of tricks and didn't want to risk anything learning new stuff. Then I got stuck in that mindset and commiting to new tricks started to feel daunting. Thinking back on it, I realize that the fact I stopped pushing may have been one of the reasons why I lost interest in unicycling at the time.

My conclusion of today's therapy session would probably be that, at least in my case, there should be a balance between novelty and betterment, to maintain the appeal. All in all, to just do what makes us happy. We're all just clowns riding around on one-wheeled bikes anyway. There's no point to it. Might at least enjoy it.

tl;dr I had a great time riding tonight. Thanks to everyone on here! 😊

Sessioned a chill new spot at the park this evening.

https://youtu.be/HVp6r5s_MgQ

On the menu: relearning hickflips and sexchanges, slowly working back towards 540's and some fun on small obstacles.

I'm starting to like the idea of filming most of my sessions, but from a learning perspective for once. While editing, I can look back at what I'm doing wrong and try to adjust on the next ride. It's nice to know that there's this raw footage somewhere online unlisted as a reference.

I guess I should fix my spokes...

Short grind sesh at the skatepark tonight. Again trying to clean up old stuff.

https://youtu.be/cSMVFRAsCkE

Anybody else has a natural stance where they need to do 'hard' spins (as per Kyle's lexicon) out of grinds? e.g. in my case, I grind LFF and my out spins are CW. 

It's ridiculous how much easier out backflips are compared to outflips, even if it's technically from a blind grind... Do you think it would be worth learning blind full outflips for the very specific case of tricks out of grinds? Full outflips out would probably eventually be possible, but currently they feel really awkward...

https://youtu.be/wk_q3Qh7f1I

This steep section of trail ranges in slope percentage from about 29 to almost 40%. After about 2/3rds of the way up, while pushing down on each pedal stroke as much as possible, the tire just lost traction and thrust me forward and off the front of the Uni. I was using the Maxxis Minion which has great grip, but I suspect maybe I had a  jet too much PSI, reducing contact with the ground. I plan to go back and try again, with a bit lower PSI. 

Btw, I don't know why the YouTube shorts thumbnail isn't showing, but the link works.

Allô!

Jakob and I try to ride together more or less once a month to keep us motivated. No pretention, no big tricks, just good fun.

Last weekend, I went to his place and we tested some new Flansberrium products. I also picked up my brand new une Jakob had just finished building, and I love it so much! It's a throwback to some of my first unicycles, almost 20 years ago.

We filmed a few clips during the ride and Jakob slapped them together. Here they are, enjoy! 😊 

https://youtu.be/3BQZ38ldP5w

I stumbled upon a cool interview with a neuroscientist (and skater) about skateboarding.

https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=H0mHX8oPbLE

I thought some of you may enjoy it as well, there are clear parallels with street unicycling.

I've never really agreed with the concept of the uniqueness of a sport community though. No offense to any of you, but I've heard people practicing scooter, inline, parkour, BMX, all say how great, special and welcoming their community was, just like I hear at every unicycling event I go to. A sense of belonging, whatever the discipline, is all that matters I would say. But apart from that, I think Huberman brings interesting techniques, for example for dealing with fear and anticipation, that I'm eager to try myself.

4×4 Unicycle Roll Challenge
A good exercise for those between beginners and pros of rolls. :)
https://youtu.be/FfJcJWkDlZ4?si=4kZMdMT_e6SKTjF2

Hey skrrrt folks, 

Here's my new vid. I've been collecting these clips for about 2 years. The title is a reference to the fact that I took a 10 year break from riding. Getting back into it with a 30+ body has been interesting.

I can't (or am too scared) to do many of the tricks / go as big as I used to. On the other hand, I've had a lot of fun learning some modern tricks that I never even knew were possible back in the day and developing a style of riding that works within my limitations. 

Enjoy!

https://youtu.be/afgTIttQeCc?si=PDgjUSGluY-HccFC

If anyone was interested in learning how to coast without having to figure out the technique from scratch, here is everything I know from practicing the skill obsessively for the past 2+ years:

https://youtu.be/kbPOb6hWMcg?si=st6m_ZUdCLQUjzot

Definitely my most high-effort video so far, but hopefully it helps make coasting click for those who are interested! 
Also, for those who already know how to coast, I would love your comments and feedback about what you agree/disagree with so I can make this a one-stop reference for the skill. Enjoy!
